responses.response: We acknowledge that the submitted EMG-IMU evidence does not support the stated claims of substantial outperformance or improved generalization. The author-visible validation catalog identifies a non-like-for-like baseline protocol, subject overlap between training and held-out data, fewer than two recorded seeds, no uncertainty estimate, no multiplicity correction, and limited validation breadth. The paper provides only the result paragraph and table for these claims. We will remove or sharply qualify the outperformance and generalization language; we do not claim a corrected result.

The submitted theorem is existential and already states its main structural cost: pair-state variables indexed by carrier and source, per-carrier hidden width equal to the total input dimension, diameter-many inter-site propagation layers, and graph-specific walk-count rescaling in the exact broadcast compilation. We acknowledge that the submission provides no parameter, runtime, sample-complexity, optimization, stability, or implementation study. We will make clear that universality does not establish practical efficiency.

The submission is not self-contained for the broad CENN framework: Definition A.2 delegates the arrow-bundle generators and Theorem A.3 imports the general UAT hypotheses. The author-visible catalog also flags several central cited identities as unconfirmed; that is not evidence of nonexistence, but we cannot present them as independently verified. What is developed in the submitted text is the finite action-groupoid account of completed OENN equivariance and a selected finite-realization statement. We will mark the general CENN material as imported background, separate it from the finite OENN/action-groupoid results developed here, and narrow the general-form wording.

We agree that the equivariance condition in Definition 2.2 is finite-group equivariance on the total direct-sum bundle, not naturality with respect to the thin poset category. The submitted paper already states that the order supplies invariant incidence relations, masks, covers, pair-state communication, and sheaf restrictions, while the action groupoid supplies equivariance; the full UAT itself uses a global broadcast. The submitted evidence does not establish a literature-wide theorem-level separation from generic finite-group equivariant UATs, especially when the automorphism group is trivial. We will narrow the framing and present the order-specific content as the structured/local architecture classes, diameter-depth compilation, and sheaf constructions rather than as a different input-output symmetry condition.

The author-visible catalog establishes a reported subject overlap and protocol mismatch, but it does not contain the exact subject-level manifest or provenance needed to explain that overlap, nor a corrected subject-disjoint rerun. Providing those items would require new research. We will not speculate and will remove or sharply qualify the affected empirical claims.

The requested subject-disjoint, protocol-matched rerun, exact split manifest, multiple-seed results, uncertainty intervals, and multiplicity analysis are not present in the submitted paper or author-visible catalog. We therefore cannot answer with a corrected performance claim or explain the reported subject overlap without new research and provenance. We will not speculate and will instead remove or sharply qualify the affected manuscript claims.

The submitted evidence does not contain complete arrow-bundle generator definitions, a self-contained proof of the general CENN UAT, or verified identities for the central CENN references flagged in the author-visible catalog. Supplying those items would require new proof or citation research, which we cannot add in rebuttal. We can only identify the finite action-groupoid/OENN statements already present, and we will revise the manuscript to make the prior-work dependency explicit and confine the claimed contribution accordingly.

The submitted evidence supports a scope distinction but not a new verified theorem-by-theorem comparison with all closest finite-group or higher-order UATs. The full theorem approximates continuous G-equivariant maps for a finite group acting on a poset-indexed bundle; the order enters through invariant relations, locality, cover propagation, and sheaf structure, not through a stronger equivariance equation. A broader novelty comparison would require new literature research, so we will not manufacture it and will instead narrow the manuscript claims.

Only the submitted structural facts are available: source-labeled pair states replicate the total input dimension at each carrier and the exact compilation uses diameter-scale propagation plus walk-count rescaling. Exact parameter count, peak memory beyond the stated hidden width, runtime, orbit/stabilizer construction cost, and measured comparisons are not provided. Answering them would require new derivations, implementation, or experiments, so we cannot supply them as rebuttal evidence and will limit the practical claim.

commitments: will: remove or sharply qualify the claims of substantial outperformance and improved generalization because the submitted evidence does not establish them
will: state that the pair-state universality result is existential and does not provide parameter, runtime, sample-complexity, optimization, or stability guarantees
will: separate the finite action-groupoid OENN correspondence developed in this submission from broader CENN definitions and UAT results imported from prior work, and narrow the general-form wording

will: clarify that OENN input-output equivariance is finite-group equivariance on the total bundle, while the poset order supplies invariant incidence, masks, covers, pair-state communication, and sheaf restriction structure
limitations_acknowledged: The submitted EMG-IMU evidence has subject-overlap, protocol-parity, repeated-seed, uncertainty, multiplicity, and breadth limitations and cannot support the stated outperformance or generalization claims.
The pair-state construction replicates the total input dimension at each carrier and uses diameter-scale propagation; no practical complexity or efficiency evaluation is established.
The broad CENN primitives and UAT are not self-contained in the submission; only the finite action-groupoid/OENN correspondence and selected finite realization statements are developed here.

The submitted full OENN UAT is a finite-group equivariant approximation theorem on a poset-indexed total bundle; the submitted evidence does not establish a theorem-level separation from all prior finite-group equivariant UATs.
